title: DNA Extraction Using the Ammonium Acetate Technique
author: Juan Carlos Illera
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.knycvfw
materials: Move the supernatant to new tubes.

steps.title: Add 50 µl of blood, 200...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µl of blood, 200 µl of a DNA extraction solution (which includes tris-HCL 30 mM ph 8, EDTA 10 mM, and 0,4% SDS), and 3 µl proteinase K. undetermined, Blood undetermined, DNA extraction solution undetermined, Proteinase K

steps.title: Add 200 µl AcNH4 4 M
steps.instruction: Add 200 µl AcNH4 4 M. Vortex and incubate 30 minutes. Shake every 10 minutes. undetermined, AcNH4 4 M 00:30:00, Vortex and incubation 00:10:00, Shake

steps.title: Add 800 µl of cold EtOH 100%
steps.instruction: Add 800 µl of cold EtOH 100%. undetermined, EtOH

steps.title: Wash the pellet with 800 µl of EtOH 70%
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet with 800 µl of EtOH 70%. undetermined, EtOH 70%

steps.title: Add 200 µl TE Buffer and...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µl TE Buffer and leave 2-3 hours in the oven at 37°C undetermined, TE Buffer 03:00:00, Oven Undetermined

steps.title: Also incubate 70°C around three hours shaking frequently
steps.instruction: Also incubate 70°C around three hours shaking frequently. Undetermined 03:00:00, Incubation
